design_philosophy.core_loop: Search for scattered Gwar concert tickets across Highland town levels, defeat enemies in beat-em-up combat, collect items for puzzle-solving
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Trial-and-error item collection and obscure puzzle-solving required to progress; combat encounters against themed enemies
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Beavis and Butt-Head characters from MTV show, attend a Gwar concert, experience the show's humor and aesthetic in interactive form
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavily licensed to MTV's Beavis and Butt-Head with faithful recreation of TV show's visual style and character humor; game's success depends entirely on player's investment in the IP. Gameplay is straightforward but uninspired—the license carries the design rather than vice versa

difficulty.lives_system: Health-based system with fatal password system glitch that can result in extremely low health at resume, making players die quickly before starting
difficulty.continues_system: Password system for progression saving, but glitched implementation causes loss of lives and near-death health states
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited agency; extensive trial-and-error required for item collection and puzzle solving reduces player control over challenge pacing. Requires both thumb speed for combat and patience for obscure item hunts

progression.upgrade_system: Item collection system for puzzle-solving progression; unclear if combat upgrades exist
progression.unlock_mechanism: Items scattered across levels must be found and collected to solve obscure puzzles that unlock level progression
replayability_hooks: Hidden/unused graphics and content suggest developer intended exploration
License appeal for show fans provides narrative replay motivation

world_building_techniques: TV show visual style recreation maintains character authenticity
Dialogue between characters (hidden behind in-game UI black bars)
Environmental storytelling through Highland town setting familiar to TV viewers
Pizza box and apartment background details grounded in show's aesthetic

faction_indicators: Gwar concert as aspirational goal (music fan identity)
Highland town setting rooted in MTV show community
danger_signals: Password system glitch causing instant death states signals poor quality assurance
Extensive trial-and-error design penalizes non-exhaustive exploration
environmental_storytelling: Recreation of TV show's aesthetic creates familiar social space for fans
Gwar licensing establishes music/concert culture context
Apartment and street environments mirror show's Highland setting

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Beavis and Butt-Head (SNES)
